 {"id":"bd-35d2","title":"E2E: headless scripted runner (custom IO + recorded inputs)","description":"Implement an E2E runner that can drive the app without a real TTY.

Core approach:
- Run the app in custom I/O mode (bubbletea `with_input`/`with_output`) so it does not touch the real terminal.
- Feed deterministic input into the program:
  - Prefer a semantic step DSL (key presses, text input, resize, mouse click/scroll).
  - Optionally support raw byte streams to exercise the InputParser explicitly.
- Capture rendered frames (strings) so tests can assert on UI output.
- Terminate deterministically (send quit key / QuitMsg; enforce a max-step guard).

Time determinism:
- Avoid wall-clock sleeps.
- Advance simulation/animations by injecting tick messages and/or running a fixed "frame pump" loop.
- Ensure scenarios can run with animations disabled to reduce flakiness.

Assertions:
- Provide helpers for:
  - `assert_contains("Jobs")` / `assert_not_contains("\x1b[")` (no-color)
  - page header checks
  - optional direct AppState assertions (if the runner has access to the final model)

Integration:
- Must plug into the structured artifact logger (`E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture`).

Acceptance:
- Scenarios are readable scripts: "press j", "open docs", "search", "quit".
- Runner failures explain *where* and *why* (step id + last frame + config snapshot).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.886754241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:16:04.460032517Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:16:04.459867219Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-366a","title":"Integration Tests: glamour + lipgloss styling","description":"Integration tests for glamour markdown with lipgloss styles.

## Purpose
Verify glamour correctly uses lipgloss for styled output
and that styles compose without corruption.

## Test Requirements

### Style Application
- [ ] glamour Style maps to lipgloss Styles
- [ ] Custom themes apply correctly
- [ ] Default themes render expected output

### Rendering Integration
- [ ] Markdown elements get correct styles
- [ ] Code blocks get syntax highlighting colors
- [ ] Nested styles (bold in italic) work
- [ ] Links have distinct styling

### Width Handling
- [ ] glamour respects lipgloss width
- [ ] Word wrap accounts for ANSI codes
- [ ] Padding/margin calculations correct

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Very wide content with styles
- [ ] Many nested style changes
- [ ] Styles across line breaks

## Success Criteria
- Styled markdown renders identically to Go charm
- No style leakage between elements
- Width calculations match expected","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:49.408003481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:20.534265045Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:20.534133109Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","integration","lipgloss","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-15c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-39bb","title":"Interactions: window resize handling + responsive reflow","description":"Make the whole app responsive.

Implementation:
- App consumes `WindowSizeMsg` and recomputes layout.
- Propagate size to pages/components (viewport/table/list widths/heights).
- Docs page re-renders markdown on width changes.

Acceptance:
- Resizing doesn’t produce broken borders or truncated controls.","notes":"Documented resize handling pattern in PageModel trait. Fixed missing AppMsg match arms. Infrastructure is in place - pages receive dimensions via view() and can detect changes for reflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:34.174618825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:51.866006422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:51.865872683Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input","resize"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39bb","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39bb","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39hl","title":"Dashboard: uptime stopwatch + incident countdown widget","description":"Exercise bubbles time components in a realistic, high-polish dashboard context.

Why:
- `bubbles::stopwatch` and `bubbles::timer` are core "real app" primitives (uptime, ETAs, countdowns).
- Time widgets also stress the update loop (tick scheduling, formatting, focus/reduce-motion behavior).

Widgets to implement:
- Uptime (stopwatch):
  - displayed as `DD:HH:MM:SS` (or similar) with compact formatting
  - clearly indicates running/paused state (pause if unfocused is optional)
- Incident SLA / next maintenance window (timer countdown):
  - counts down to an upcoming event
  - visual emphasis as it nears zero (color + subtle animation)

Integration:
- Drive updates via bubbletea tick/every (no manual sleeps).
- Respect global toggles:
  - reduce-motion / `--no-animations` (no flashy pulsing)
  - focus/blur (optionally throttle when unfocused)

Acceptance:
- Widgets look polished and update smoothly.
- No excessive CPU usage (tick frequency is reasonable).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:24.377240967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T03:28:02.945483198Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T03:28:02.945417966Z","close_reason":"Implemented uptime stopwatch and SLA countdown widgets in dashboard status bar: 1) Stopwatch-format uptime display (DD:HH:MM:SS), 2) SLA countdown with color-coded urgency (normal/warning/critical/breached), 3) Visual emphasis as SLA approaches zero (bold + warning icon). Timer updates respect the tick-based simulation (10 ticks = 1 second). Already integrated with reduce-motion via existing animation infrastructure.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","stopwatch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39my","title":"demo_showcase data: async job runner demo (AsyncCmd)","description":"When the `bubbletea/async` feature is enabled, demonstrate a realistic async workload pipeline using `AsyncCmd` (non-blocking background work that produces messages).

Why:
- Async is a headline capability: it proves TUIs can do real I/O work without blocking input.
- The showcase should prove both *correctness* (no races/panics) and *ergonomics* (clean update logic).

What to demonstrate (in-app):
- I/O-like operations:
  - “fetch metrics”, “deploy service”, “load docs index”, “export logs”, etc.
  - each operation returns a message that updates state + triggers UI feedback
- Concurrency:
  - multiple AsyncCmds can run concurrently (batch)
  - results reconcile safely into the model
- Cancellation semantics (best-effort):
  - bubbletea AsyncCmd itself doesn’t inherently cancel tasks; demonstrate cancellation via:
    - per-job generation IDs / cancellation tokens
    - ignoring late results for canceled jobs
    - UI shows “canceled” and stops progress updates

Implementation notes:
- Feature-gate the async runner with `cfg(feature = "async")` and provide a sync fallback path so the demo still works without async.
- Prefer deterministic delays in test mode (e.g., fixed durations or seeded RNG) so E2E is stable.

Acceptance:
- Async mode feels like a real app doing background work while remaining responsive.
- No input blocking; no terminal corruption; no runaway task spawning.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - async pipeline updates state correctly (use tokio test + deterministic time where feasible)
  - cancellation token/generation logic ignores late results
  - batch/sequence composition behaves as expected
- E2E:
  - at least one scenario runs with async enabled (can be a separate CI job if needed) and asserts job progression + completion.

Notes:
- Keep the API surface small: most of the complexity should live in demo_showcase domain state, not in glue.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyGlen","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:52.895189964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:13:57.578041605Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:13:57.577870877Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
